CN-US.tariffNotes: Section 301 tariffs may apply (25% on List 1-3, 7.5% on List 4A). Check HTS code classification.

CN-EU.tariffNotes: EU Common External Tariff applies. Anti-dumping duties on steel, solar panels, e-bikes.

US-EU.tariffNotes: No comprehensive FTA. MFN tariffs apply. Some retaliatory tariffs on steel/aluminum.

EU-US.tariffNotes: MFN tariffs. Wine and spirits subject to periodic retaliatory tariffs.

JP-US.tariffNotes: US-Japan Trade Agreement provides some tariff reductions on agriculture and industrial goods.

US-CN.tariffNotes: Retaliatory tariffs on US goods (25% on $110B of goods). Agricultural products subject to quota.

DE-US.tariffNotes: 2.5% tariff on passenger vehicles, 25% on light trucks (Chicken Tax). Machinery generally 0-5%.
restrictions: Vehicles must meet FMVSS and EPA standards

IN-US.tariffNotes: India removed from GSP in 2019. MFN tariffs apply. Textiles subject to specific duty rates.

US-JP.tariffNotes: US-Japan Trade Agreement reduces tariffs on beef, pork, some agricultural products. Industrial goods vary.

AU-US.tariffNotes: Australia-US FTA (AUSFTA) eliminates most tariffs. Some agricultural products have TRQs.

CN-JP.tariffNotes: RCEP provides preferential tariffs. Tariff elimination phased over 20 years for sensitive items.
restrictions: Strict pesticide residue limits on food

KR-US.tariffNotes: KORUS FTA eliminates most tariffs. Auto tariff phase-out completed. Textiles rules of origin apply.

US-MX.tariffNotes: USMCA duty-free for qualifying goods. Rules of origin stricter than NAFTA for autos (75% regional content).

MX-US.tariffNotes: USMCA duty-free for qualifying goods. Avocados/tomatoes subject to seasonal monitoring.

GB-US.tariffNotes: No FTA post-Brexit. MFN tariffs apply. Scotch whisky subject to periodic tariff disputes.

documents: Certificate of Origin (Form A for GSP if reinstated)
VN-US.tariffNotes: MFN tariffs apply. Anti-dumping duties on shrimp, catfish, and steel. Textile-specific duties.
restrictions: Wood products require Lacey Act compliance

SG-US.tariffNotes: US-Singapore FTA eliminates virtually all tariffs. Very liberal rules of origin.

BR-EU.tariffNotes: Mercosur-EU agreement pending ratification. Currently MFN tariffs. High tariffs on ethanol, sugar, beef.

AE-IN.tariffNotes: India-UAE CEPA reduces tariffs on 80% of tariff lines. Gold and jewelry preferential rates.
restrictions: Gold import subject to RBI regulations

US-CA.tariffNotes: USMCA duty-free for qualifying goods. Dairy, poultry, eggs under supply management (high over-quota tariffs).
restrictions: Cultural goods (books) may have content rules

documentDescriptions.Commercial Invoice: Seller's invoice showing goods, quantities, prices, terms of sale (Incoterms).
documentDescriptions.Packing List: Detailed breakdown of package contents, weights, and dimensions.
documentDescriptions.Bill of Lading: Contract of carriage between shipper and carrier for sea freight.
documentDescriptions.Airway Bill: Contract of carriage for air freight (non-negotiable).
documentDescriptions.Certificate of Origin: Document certifying the country where goods were manufactured.
documentDescriptions.ISF 10+2: Importer Security Filing required 24 hours before US-bound vessel loading.
documentDescriptions.EEI: Electronic Export Information filed via AES for US exports over $2,500.
documentDescriptions.EUR.1: Movement certificate proving EU/partner country origin for preferential tariffs.
documentDescriptions.Phytosanitary Certificate: Government certificate that plants/plant products are pest-free.
documentDescriptions.Fumigation Certificate: Proof that wood packaging has been heat-treated or fumigated (ISPM-15).
